entries.name: Admiral Seruun

fields.label: Role
fields.value: Senior military commander; tactician; reluctant warrior

sections.body: Full title: Admiral Seruun of Kelan'tai, Commander of Accord Fleet Group Vesta, leading from the bridge of the *Aegis Reborn*. A Torelvan who embodies the weight of duty — slow to speak, slower to act, unstoppable once set in motion. Holds the line as politics crumble and ancient powers reawaken.

Massive even by Torelvan standards; skin like polished basalt laced with faint blue veins that pulse with harmonic grounding. Heavy commander's armor with tactical resonance baffles. (Book 1 was revised — the earlier "gaunt face" line is removed; the classic Torelvan frame stands.)
A strategist of immense patience and few words; voice low and slow, "like the settling of mountains." Torelvan creed: strength is for service, not domination. Distrusts emotion as a guide.
Senses the disturbance Lyra brings; troubled by James's link to resonance more than he admits.

- **Elyon Tarsis:** A trusted ally; Seruun often pushes for more decisive action than Elyon will take. Their dynamic balances vision and discipline.
- **Karix:** Sees in him a perversion of purpose — a warrior who betrayed strength by turning it into a weapon of tyranny.
- **Lyra:** Doesn't understand her, but senses the disturbance her presence brings; she is no longer merely a child in his eyes.
- **James Carter:** A wildcard he keeps his distance from but watches closely; the human's link to resonance troubles him more than he admits.

~430 standard cycles; a Torelvan born on the gravity-dense settled world of Kelan'tai. (The Torelvans have settled multiple worlds; Kael'Zor is the original homeworld.) Seeks no glory, only preservation; though he often disagrees with the Council's delays, he obeys — until duty requires defiance.
- RESOLVED (2026-06-15): the "gaunt face" line was removed in a Book 1 revision; no conflict remains.
Book 1 (Admiral Seruun; revised text removing the "gaunt face" line). Merged from former long-form character source (2026-06-16).

entries.name: Admiral Voss Enlarn

fields.label: Role
fields.value: Primary military commander under Karix; Vanguard Commander of the Spear Fleets; enforcer, zealot

sections.body: The blade in Karix's hand: cold, ruthless, surgically precise. Commands the Spear Fleets that sweep across the stars subjugating resistance and rooting out anomalies. Driven not by ambition but by fanatical loyalty to Karix's vision. Title is **"Admiral"** (not "High Admiral"); first name **Voss**.

Brutal augmentations: face partly replaced with reflective lattice-metal, spine reinforced with exposed harmonic regulators that hum near resonance shifts. Jet-black Dominion carapace armor. One real eye, one adaptive sensor. Moves like a weapon unsheathed.
Clipped authority, never wastes a word. To him empathy is weakness and hesitation betrayal. A true believer — sees Karix as "resonance incarnate."
Sees Lyra as the most dangerous element in the galaxy because she is "something unwritten"; views Earth as a wound to be cauterized.

- **Karix:** More than a master — to Enlarn, Karix is a god; his every command sacred, his silence a test of faith.
- **General Kael (deceased):** Despised his conflicted motives; saw his death as necessary, "a fracture sealed."
- **Lyra:** A paradox that defies all Dominion models; the most dangerous element in the galaxy not for her power but because she is "something unwritten."
- **Elyon Tarsis:** A diplomat whose existence offends him; he believes words are "the last refuge of the weak."
- **Admiral Seruun:** A worthy obstacle whose strength he respects, but would see shattered if he stands in the way.

~130 standard cycles. Full style: Vanguard Commander of the Dominion Spear Fleets. Where Karix dreams in silence, Enlarn acts — swiftly, without mercy, always in harmony with the Emperor's will. His goal is to neutralize all unquantifiable threats to Dominion order, starting with the remaining Thal'Seran fragments and their human entanglements.
- In Book 2 he "knocks at the wall from outside" the Sol Lock (contrast to Vasik inside).

Book 1 (Admiral Voss Enlarn); Book 2 (Enlarn outside the Sol Lock). Merged from former long-form character source (2026-06-16). NAMING NOTE (style-guide): "Admiral," not "High Admiral"; "Voss" is HIS first name (not Hadrek's).

threads: In Book 2 he "knocks at the wall from outside" the Sol Lock (contrast to Vasik inside).

entries.name: Aelri Venn-Orin

fields.label: Affiliation
fields.value: Galactic Accord — Embedded Deep Observer (active cover; Conditional Tier-6; file flagged "emotional drift")

fields.label: Role
fields.value: The Accord's watcher on Earth; Lyra's protector; increasingly an advocate against the Accord

sections.body: A Virellan Watcher of the Accord's silent non-interference echelon, assigned to monitor the dormant Thal'Seran structure (the Veilcore) on Earth. Operates under the cover identity **Elian Verne ("Miss Verne")**, a resource assistant at Pine Valley Elementary. An active, credentialed Accord agent throughout Book 1; by the book's end she steps out of cover, stands before the Accord Council under her true name, and argues for Earth ("what's happening on Earth isn't chaos — it's music").

As Verne: appears human, mid-forties, silver-streaked dark hair, plain glasses, slightly "out of phase." Faint glow of Virellan symbiotics along her spine; veins shimmer and voice carries harmonic undertones when resonance surges.
Cautious, brilliant, weathered by time; empathy disciplined into watchfulness until Lyra and James crack it open. Speaks Kael's name with open contempt.

Holds the Accord's secret of Earth; the Accord holds her clearance. Believes the Thal'Seran hid more than memory here — they sealed hope. How long she has truly been embedded is deliberately obscured (Kael's analysis: closer to the girl "far longer than the Accord admitted").

- **Lyra:** The child she was sent to observe and now chooses to protect, even from her own chain of command. In Lyra she sees both the past she archived and a future no doctrine predicted.
- **James Carter:** A reluctant ally who becomes the hinge of her worldview. She defends him before the Council: "He's bonded by presence. By trust."
- **Dr. Evelyn Roan:** Mutual wariness hardening into alliance; Evelyn doesn't trust her but listens anyway.
- **The Accord:** Her employer, her flag, and increasingly her opponent — they hold her clearance; she holds their secret.
- **Kael (deceased):** Identified her cover before his death; she speaks of his betrayal with open contempt.
- **Sheriff Matthews:** A local complication she managed carefully — a "blunt but useful tool" others were also moving on the board.

Over 200 standard cycles (estimated). Long embedded on Earth; her cover began at Pine Valley Elementary when Mrs. Goodwin's pregnancy turned (soft gray sweater, clean slacks, a clipped lanyard, a resume that mentions Vermont). Lyra clocks her immediately — "the helper lady, the one with too-smooth steps" — and feels her resonance before hearing her name. Kael identified her cover before his death; even Dominion intelligence noted she had "stayed on Earth too long, begun to understand too much." Her closing argument to the Council favors recognition over containment — "we start listening back."

- How long she has actually been embedded (deliberately obscured).
- Possible distant kinship with Shade-Lord Myra Venn ("echo-kin").
- Her standing on record against her own institution's containment doctrine.
Book 1 (Aelri Venn-Orin / "Elian Verne"); cross-refs in James, Lyra, Evelyn, Kael, Myra entries. Merged from former long-form character source (2026-06-16).

entries.name: Alan Matthews

fields.label: Role
fields.value: Local law enforcement; conflicted antagonist; unwitting pawn; host of Meraith's seed

sections.body: Long-serving Sheriff of Pine Valley. A man of rules, worn by years of compromise, whose grip slips as strange events spiral around James Carter and Lyra. Routine suspicion turns into paranoia, manipulation, and exposure to alien resonance. At the Book 1 climax, Meraith plants a "small, imperceptible, carefully tuned" seed in him — the narration promises it "would grow."

Weathered, commanding presence: graying hair, lined face, build hardened by labor and patrol. Tired but sharp eyes. After crash-site resonance exposure he hears tones no one else hears; his dreams fill with light and song.

Trained to believe in chains of command and simple guilt/innocence; chasing control in an unraveling world. Increasingly fractured and unreliable to himself as the Irix influence accelerates his spiral.
By the end of Book 1 he no longer trusts his own memories. Unaware he now hosts Meraith's seed.

An unnamed past tragedy lingers and colors his decisions (perhaps a lost child, a failed case, or a moment of cowardice). His only deputy, Mason Garrett, increasingly the voice of reason, begins to question his choices — a widening rift. (The county is not named on-page.)

- The seed in him is a live Book 2 thread; in Book 2 Rhel Vasik finds it, feeds it, and steers him via neural interface (Ch. 9 reveal).
- His unnamed past tragedy.
Book 1 (Alan Matthews; Meraith's seed at the climax); Book 2 (Vasik feeds/steers the seed). Merged from former long-form character source (2026-06-16).

entries.name: Dorsey

fields.label: Affiliation
fields.value: Pine Valley townsfolk (local church, dissenting half)

fields.label: Role
fields.value: The "closed sky is a lid" voice; leads the congregants who break with Pastor Lindqvist

sections.body: A Pine Valley man who, in the second week of the seal, stands up in the third pew and rejects Pastor Lindqvist's "mercy" reading of the closed sky — "it was no hand, it was a *lid*" — asking which Scripture has the Lord sealing the righteous in *with* the thing they're meant to be sealed away from. His faction leaves to meet in the Hagens' barn.
[Not described.]
Blunt, scriptural, unafraid to challenge the pastor in front of the room.
[New in Book 2; no Book 1 appearance.]
- Whether the split congregation reconciles or hardens as the cordon arrives.
Manuscript CHAPTER_10.

entries.name: Queen Elara Elenai

fields.label: Affiliation
fields.value: House Elenai, Sovereign of the Thal'Seran

fields.label: Role
fields.value: Queen-consort; mother of Lyra; final steward and tuner of the Earth Veilcore's harmonic lock

fields.label: Status (alive/dead)
fields.value: DECEASED — died aboard the *Celestis Vanaar* in the Dominion ambush

sections.body: The soul of House Elenai — the last Harmonic Architect of the Thal'Seran. She ruled through resonance rather than force, capable of aligning structures and weaving memory into the quantum strands of matter. Where Theron was the protector, Elara was the guiding light. A **steward and final tuner** of the Earth Veilcore — not its architect (the Veilcore predates Elenai rule). Her essence survives in the pendant she placed on Lyra and the instructions that saved her child.
Luminous even among the Thal'Seran; hair like starlight, faintly radiant skin, presence that "quieted rooms like a chord resolving." Robes interlaced with living crystal tuned to her inner harmony.
Fiercely intelligent, compassionate — "the quiet storm." Gentle but unyielding. Believed legacy is responsibility, not lineage: "To leave behind not your name, but your echo."

Crafted the final harmonic seed — a lock keyed to bloodlines that can stabilize the song ("Elara Elenai's design signature. Final lattice encoding") — into Lyra's living resonance, and prepared the hidden pod ("Only if everything breaks, little star"; "Behind the biosensor wall. Say your name").

Foresaw the fall and the betrayal; moved in silence as steward. Once trusted Kael with her daughter's protection — "he broke the song she had placed in him." Saw Earth not just as a hiding place but as a mystery — "a world that sang back to her." The Veilcore's harmonics carry traces of her voice, awaiting reactivation through Lyra; in her final recorded image, encoded in the Veilcore's deep memory, she sings without sound. Aelri Venn-Orin (Elian Verne), a former Accord observer who admired Elara's grace from afar, still mourns her — "because Elara understood even the watchers needed someone to believe in."
- Her encoded image/voice in the Veilcore's deep memory, awaiting reactivation through Lyra.
Book 1 (Queen Elara Elenai; the pendant, hidden pod, and biosensor-wall instruction). Merged from former long-form character source (2026-06-16). Per canon rule #6: steward/tuner, not architect.

entries.name: Elder Zyn
entries.detail: Echari (species entry not yet written — stub; Stephen to define)

fields.label: Species
fields.value: Echari (species entry not yet written — stub; Stephen to define)

fields.label: Role
fields.value: The Council's oldest voice; mystic, archivist, conscience

sections.body: Elder (Archivist) Zyn of the Echari is the oldest member present in the Accord Council scenes of Book 1 — a mystic and archivist who speaks rarely and lands heavily. Zyn reframes the Earth debate toward meaning: Earth "was not chosen for strategic reasons. It was chosen because it was forgotten. Because it sings — not with complexity, but with something far more dangerous: harmony without intent."
[not stated in sources]
Speaks rarely, weightily; the table's conscience.

First at the table to name the stakes of the human convergence ("If the human's presence can quiet the Song… it may also one day bend it") and the first to say aloud what the Syntari intend: "They will silence that song before it spreads."
[not stated in sources]
- The Echari species, Zyn's age, and Zyn's relationship to the Records Division / Accord memory archives are not yet established (Stephen to define).

entries.name: Elyon Tarsis

fields.label: Role
fields.value: Diplomat; mediator; the Accord's central political voice on the Earth crisis

sections.body: The luminous, eloquent face of the Galactic Accord — a phototropic Virellan empath and master of interspecies diplomacy from Virellis Prime. As leader of the Outer Reach Division (Sector 17 / Sol containment zone), he fronts the Earth crisis. He/him throughout Book 1. Holds **both** titles "High Representative" and "High Chancellor" (both canonical, used as fits the scene; some Accord species render the two as one office in Diplomatic Common).

Tall, lean, translucent skin that glows faintly under strong emotion (pale violet and steel-gray in tempered resolve); light-reactive filament hair shifting by mood; grown crystalline garments. Eyes storm-gray in Book 1, shifting toward opalescence under strong resonance.
Pacifist by philosophy but not naïve; believes in harmonic equilibrium. Diplomacy as a symphony he conducts — subtle, exacting. Withholds judgment until all truths are known.
Can perceive emotional and harmonic shifts in others — "hears" dissonance in a person. Senses something ancient and unfinished in Lyra; watches James as possible anomaly or keystone.

~90 standard cycles (young by Virellan standards); rose quickly through Accord ranks by empathy and foresight, not force or ambition. Seeks to preserve peace through understanding; views the resurgence of Thal'Seran resonance on Earth as a potential turning point, while fearing what resonance awakens. Often the calming center amid the Accord Council's chaos. Views Karix not merely as a tyrant but as a cautionary tale of a mind unmoored from empathy; sees Lyra as a possible bridge between old wounds and future hope.
- Whether James is anomaly or keystone (his open question).

Book 1 (Elyon Tarsis); leader of the Outer Reach Division. Merged from former long-form character source (2026-06-16). Style-guide: holds both "High Representative" and "High Chancellor." entries.name: Emily

sections.body: James's wife, who died of illness before the events of Book 1. Her loss — together with a lost child — is the grief James lives inside at the story's start. She is present through absence: the things he keeps and won't look at.
[Not described on the page.]
[Glimpsed only in James's memory — e.g., laughing from the hallway "with a mug of tea and too many opinions" while he painted the nursery.]

She and James painted the nursery cream "the spring before she got sick." They lost a baby. After her death James kept the cabin as a kind of shrine (the mantel: a folded flag, a small box, a dust ring). In Book 2 he keeps an unfinished letter addressed to "Em" — see Open Threads and decision #6.
- The unfinished letter to Emily (decision #6, locked): the Book 2 ending shows James finishing it. Drafting sub-question — do we see the words, or only the act of finishing? (Leaning: the act only.)
Book 1 (*The Silence Between Stars*) — Emily / the lost baby / the mantel; Manuscript CHAPTER_02 (the "Em" letter), CHAPTER_06 (mantel imagery); Meta/Book2_Decisions_Log.md #6.

entries.name: Emperor-Dominant Vol Karix

fields.label: Role
fields.value: Primary antagonist; tyrant visionary; galactic manipulator

sections.body: The cold, calculating ruler of the Dominion Empire — once a brilliant Syntari strategist, now more will than flesh, seated on the Throne of the Dominant (recursive cybernetic roots and harmonic control). His mind is interwoven with the Dominion's core systems; he perceives the galaxy in resonance, probability, and control.

More machine than man: skeletal form armored in void-metal, woven with harmonic spines; eyes are lenses of void-glass flickering with quantum threads. Moves only when necessary, preferring to extend his will through agents and machines.
Order through augmentation; perfection through domination; destiny through design. Ruthlessly intelligent, emotionally distant; speaks with quiet authority, never raising his voice.

Fears the unknown — variables he cannot quantify. Destroyed House Elenai and the Thal'Seran because their mastery of resonance defied calculation; now obsessed with erasing Lyra and the awakened Earth Veilcore.

Conspired with Kael to destroy House Elenai, later made him a general — then executed Kael personally in the Throne Spire after the Earth operation collapsed ("a failure wrapped in consequence"); to Karix, loyalty was always conditional. Seeks not merely conquest but certainty — a universe where nothing escapes his grasp. Treats the Accord as antagonists in name but pawns in his mind, manipulating their hesitation and fractured politics. Fears Lyra's potential more than he admits.

Born into a Syntari sub-caste of tactical architects, Karix showed early resonance instability: his harmonic signature spiked unpredictably. Rather than being purged (the standard Syntari response to flaw), he was studied — some believed his instability could evolve into control. The affinity carried him into elite experimental programs testing quantum-lattice modulation as a command interface, where he encountered pre-Dominion resonance architecture left by the [[thal-seran]].

In a near-dead Thal'Seran cradle-fragment in the [[hollow-drift]] he found a cryptic seed labeled **17-K**, encoded in the [[the-first-voice|First Voice]] and bearing the crest of [[house-elenai]]. It referenced a veil-bearing world whose coordinates were sealed behind a harmonic cipher only a bloodline heir could unlock. He spent decades decoding it and building a resonance engine to simulate access, coming to believe the Thal'Seran had not died out but retreated — and that Earth was their hidden refuge.

He launched a coup against the Dominion Council, using synthetic resonance to overwhelm their command harmonics, and seized the throne lattice — merging his mind with the Dominion Throne (a living neural network fusing memory, power, and will). He casts himself not as the Thal'Seran legacy's preserver but its corrector. His vow on the seed: "If the seed awakens… I will silence it. And if the song remembers… I will rewrite it."
- His drive to erase "the final anomaly" (Lyra / the Veilcore) propels the Dominion threat into Book 2.

Book 1 (Emperor-Dominant Vol Karix; the Throne Spire execution of Kael). Rise-to-power backstory (the 17-K seed, the coup, the Throne merge, the vow) merged from the former "Vol Karix — The Rise of the Dominant" lore source. Merged from former long-form source (2026-06-16).

entries.name: Dr. Evelyn Roan

fields.label: Affiliation
fields.value: Roan Family Medical Clinic, Pine Valley

sections.body: Pine Valley's only general practitioner, running the Roan Family Medical Clinic (sign: "Roan Family Medicine"). Steady, smart, emotionally perceptive. When Lyra lands on her exam table — bleeding light, speaking a non-human language — Evelyn chooses to protect the child even at the cost of laws and career.
No-nonsense professionalism: lab coat, ponytail, rolled sleeves. Kind but alert eyes; efficient movements. In private, weariness and old grief show.
Pragmatic but deeply empathetic; a scientist by training, caretaker at heart. Quiet, sharp wit used to disarm tension. No patience for arrogance, cruelty, or "bullies in uniform."
Knows Lyra's non-human physiology; shares the secret with James. Wary of Verne but listens because Verne has answers. Carries her own folded grief.

Knew James before his life collapsed; feels lingering responsibility toward him, having seen his pain and the fragile hope Lyra gives him. To Lyra she is first a medical mystery, then a scared, gifted child she chooses to believe in. She has watched the change in Sheriff Matthews and doesn't like it. She feels a quiet burden for the town's people but no longer believes the town would understand — or forgive — what's happening.
- Her own unspecified past grief (kept "neatly folded").
Book 1 (Dr. Evelyn Roan; clinic name "Roan Family Medicine"). Merged from former long-form character source (2026-06-16).

entries.name: General Kael

fields.label: Affiliation
fields.value: Formerly House Elenai / Thal'Seran High Command; defected to the Dominion

fields.label: Role
fields.value: Turncoat general; architect of the Elenai betrayal; Dominion field commander

fields.label: Status (alive/dead)
fields.value: DECEASED — executed by Emperor-Dominant Vol Karix in the Throne Spire (Book 1, "To Be Seen Again"); no redemption arc

sections.body: General Kael Virelan of House Virelan was the Elenai's most trusted commander — "their sword. Their shield. Their strategist" — who fed Karix the Elenai's secrets (resonance maps, bloodline signatures, encryption) and delivered the *Celestis Vanaar* into ambush. He survived the Vanaar's fall, reported the strike clean ("no pods launched"), and took command within the Dominion. He directed the Earth operation and finally initiated **Protocol Virex** to sever James from Lyra and burn out the Veilcore. Summoned to the Throne Spire after failure, he was executed.

Tall, elegant, once armored in living resonance-plate; radiated a subtle resonance visible to the attuned. In Dominion service: a neural relay at the collar, formal court robes; by his final scene, armor scorched from failed deployments.

Once defined by discipline, honor, loyalty — but in the end driven by fear that the Thal'Seran were doomed. Believed in control over chaos, order over legacy; "never stopped hearing the old harmonics." Lyra remembers his voice bending the colors of the song.
Held the Elenai's codes and ceremonies. By Book 1's end he knew he had failed: "The song has begun. And I've already failed to stop it once."

Made secret contact with Karix out of desperation; betrayed from his own fear, knowing nothing of the Accord's Old Fracture (canon — see Open Threads). His betrayal is shown twice in Book 1: lived through Lyra's eyes in Chapter 1, and again in the Veilcore vision near the book's end (two accounts of the same event, pre-Chapter 1). Karix didn't need to convince him to betray — only to offer a path Kael believed would preserve something. Theron and Elara were his sovereigns, both of whom died aboard the *Celestis Vanaar* in the assault he made possible; Elara once trusted him with her daughter's protection. To Kael, the Accord was too slow and fragmented to stop what was coming; he believed he was doing what they could not. Verne names his act: "That wasn't survival. That was desecration."
- The Old Fracture acted *upstream* of Kael (he knew nothing of it) — the present-day Accord traitor is a Book 3 reveal (see [[the-old-fracture]]).

[not stated in sources]
[not stated in sources]

Refers to Myra Venn only as a "threat vector unclassifiable." Rhel Vasik respects but does not fear him ("Rhun breaks things; Vasik solves them"); unlike Karix or Vasik, Rhun is described as a worshipper of the Dominion ideal.
Reprogrammed Meraith centuries ago via resonance-suppression technology and Dominion subroutine cores.

entries.name: James Carter

fields.label: Role
fields.value: Protagonist; reluctant guardian; emotional anchor; the convergence

sections.body: Reclusive mechanic outside Pine Valley, Minnesota; owner of Carter Auto & Machine. A former Marine who lost his wife Emily and unborn child and withdrew from the world, until he finds the child Lyra in the snow and chooses to protect her. The Veilcore acknowledges his presence; he is "the convergence" — the human whose bond with Lyra becomes decisive.
Tall, broad-shouldered, worn by grief and labor. Short-cropped hair, perpetual stubble, tired eyes. Always practical clothes, dirty hands. Walks like a soldier.
Stoic, emotionally guarded, fiercely private; doesn't talk about the past. Slow to accept the truth about Lyra, but once committed, commits completely. Speaks plainly, means every word.
Doesn't understand resonance, prophecy, or galactic politics — understands love and sacrifice. Still talks to his late wife; still wears the ring.

Lost wife Emily and unborn child; left the Marines; retreated into mechanic work and grief before Lyra's arrival. Lyra reawakens a sense of purpose he thought died with Emily; his goal becomes to protect her at all costs — not because she's powerful or alien, but because "she's his," the child the universe gave him after taking everything else. Values quiet loyalty over grand gestures.

- His "convergence" nature and how/why the Veilcore acknowledges him (Book 2 deep-root payoff).
- Targeted by Rhel Vasik, who does not understand how a human interferes with resonance flow.
Book 1 (James Carter; "bonded by presence" per Aelri Venn-Orin's Council defense); Book 2 (the convergence made physical against Vasik). Merged from former long-form character source (2026-06-16).

threads: His "convergence" nature and how/why the Veilcore acknowledges him (Book 2 deep-root payoff).

entries.name: Ke'lar Vex

fields.label: Affiliation
fields.value: Formerly Accord Records Division; now unaligned (Sovereign, Unbound)

fields.label: Role
fields.value: Kelari sovereign-exile archivist; witness to the falsified Vanaar record; the "watcher in the woods"

sections.body: The first **Kelari sovereign** — a modular, insectoid being who broke from hive-bound identity to retain forbidden resonance data. Once a trusted Accord archivist, Vex now operates autonomously, obsessed with the true history behind the fall of House Elenai and the activation of Earth's Veilcore. The Accord deems her an active anomaly: too valuable to silence, too dangerous to control. (Renamed Ke'lar Vex per Stephen, June 2026; the name "Zyn" now belongs to the Echari elder — see [[elder-zyn]].)

Tall, segmented Kelari observation-caste form: four manipulators, multi-faceted compound eyes, dim thoracic resonance filaments, black chitin with adaptive lattice plates. Movement smooth but disjointed; sporadic phase flicker.

Formal, clipped, occasionally poetic; dry humor and metaphorical reasoning; references banned texts and Terran scripture. Cognition fragmented but stable. Creed: "Memory is Responsibility"; falsification is cultural death.

Investigating the Celestis Vanaar signal anomaly post-catastrophe, she recovered harmonic data inconsistent with official Accord timelines; her report was sealed and her access revoked. What she found was neither error nor Dominion propaganda but a deliberate **Accord-internal forgery** — the official account of the Vanaar's fall doctored to read as pure Dominion aggression and to erase the Accord's own foreknowledge and complicity (see [[the-old-fracture]]). She did not drift from the Accord's values; she stayed truer to them than the Accord stayed to itself. Holds fragmentary First Voice codices. Rift-Class: can vanish into a resonance field; harmonic cloaking renders detection unreliable. Displays non-compliance with Accord doctrine but has not acted with hostility. Field note (Vex-Log 112.4.α): "Earth is not an evolutionary mistake. It is a harmonic fulcrum. It bends probability *without unraveling.* That is not chaos. That is *intention.*" Suspected to have intercepted the subharmonic pulse "sung" by Lyra. Entered Sol under a stealth envelope days before the lock fell — and is now sealed inside, with the two things she has chased for a lifetime: Lyra (who carries the living memory of the fall) and the Veilcore (which recorded it).

Cognition fragmented but highly stable; capable of non-linear associative recall; frequently converses with data constructs or memory echoes. Suffers periodic identity bleed from partial de-synchronization during hive severance, and displays behaviors approximating empathy, especially toward beings with unresolved memory trauma (notably Lyra).
Served 113 cycles as a field archivist across failed-contact worlds; severed from her hive to preserve the corrupted data stream — has remained sovereign ever since.

The "watcher in the woods" payoff (Ch. 8), arriving as archivist rather than agent, who delivers the bombshell that someone inside the Accord fed Kael's coup. In the descent (Act 2B) she burns her stealth envelope — her most precious asset — to get the party through the cordon, spent for Lyra, and witnesses the deep root.
- Through Book 2 she names the crime but **not the individual** — the present-day Old Fracture face is a Book 3 reveal.

Book 2 (Ke'lar Vex; the "watcher in the woods" / Ch. 8; the falsified Vanaar record, decision #7; the descent). Merged from former long-form character source (2026-06-16). Renamed Ke'lar Vex per Stephen, June 2026.

entries.name: Lena Marsh

fields.label: Role
fields.value: CPS agent investigating Lyra's guardianship; the external pressure on James's cover story

fields.label: Status (alive/dead)
fields.value: Alive; thread left open ("Questions don't sleep")

sections.body: A county Child Protective Services agent who shows up at the cabin on an "anonymous concern regarding a minor in [James's] care" — no school records, no registered guardianship, remote living conditions. She reviews the forged paperwork (state-seal copies, a scrambled adoption form, a homeschool affidavit), and is sharp enough to notice what the cover is meant to hide: that Lyra barely blinks. Lyra unnerves her in turn ("Your voice is very flat. Like glass. No echo."). She does not clear James — "Verify the agency. Cross-check the school affidavit. Until then, Mr. Carter, don't leave the county." — and returns again before Book 1's end, with Evelyn Roan stepping in as Lyra's declared advocate and physician.
Pleasant in a clipped, practiced, bureaucratic way; badge, clipboard/tablet, scarf.
Mild, procedural, persistent. Wields politeness "like a scalpel." Notices details.
Suspects the paperwork doesn't hold up; does not know what Lyra actually is. Her scrutiny is the human-bureaucratic threat that runs underneath the cover story.
[Book 1 county official; no stated background.]

- **Live thread (carried into Book 2):** her investigation is unresolved. Lyra's own line closes it open — "She will try again. Questions don't sleep." In Ch. 12 the escalation arrives: her winter CPS complaint and guardianship flag are now folded into the federal cordon's file on the Carter cabin, the thin paperwork used as the crack to justify reaching the girl. Whether Marsh herself reappears or is simply absorbed by the federal apparatus is open.
Book 1 (CPS visit: "Lena Marsh. Child Protective Services"; "don't leave the county"; the blinking; "Questions don't sleep"); Manuscript Book 1 reference text.

threads: **Live thread (carried into Book 2):** her investigation is unresolved. Lyra's own line closes it open — "She will try again. Questions don't sleep." entries.name: Lyra

fields.label: Role
fields.value: Last heir of House Elenai; resonance key; adopted daughter of James Carter

sections.body: A child (apparent age 6 in Book 1) who arrived in deep Minnesota snow with no record and silver, mirror-like eyes. The last living heir of House Elenai, encoded with the Thal'Seran's final harmonic seed; her presence wakes the Veilcore. Cradle-name "Lyra"; full First Voice name ⟨Sael'ynne Elenai'Vareth ti'Sorael⟩, spoken aloud once on-page to open the Vanaar's biosensor wall.
Pale skin with faint internal luminescence under stress; silver-white hair like frost; eyes that shimmer like mirrors reflecting starlight. Usually barefoot; moves silently.

Perceptive, emotionally attuned, alien in unsettling ways. Mimics and echoes language to learn it; hums when calm; glows when she dreams. Curious, cautious, brave; fears abandonment above all. Wants to be Lyra, not a legacy.

**Book 2 voice (LOCKED, Stephen 2026-06-15):** mirror end of Book 1 — **Stage 3**. She is *good at English* but speaks in poetic, strange, displaced phrasing; this reflects her alien *cognition*, not weak English. Keep synesthetic metaphor-as-literal-description and slightly-off syntax; still drops into First Voice for what English can't hold. **Apparent age: 6** (childlike and ancient at once; not precocious-cute). **The Sol Lock does not change her voice** — it only kills FTL travel/comms (decision #1); write her resonance as in Book 1. (Full texture + examples: `../Meta/VoiceStyleGuide_Book2.md`.)

Doesn't understand her own nature yet, but is "remembering" — fragments of the First Voice and memories not her own. Abilities (uncontrolled): resonance sensitivity, light manipulation, the First Voice (Veyariin Shal'keth), harmonic lock that wakes Thal'Seran tech. Wears the pendant Elara placed on her ("Blue is when it listens").

sections.h: Cover story & public identity (Book 1 — keep present in Book 2)
sections.body:
James built a two-layer cover to pass Lyra off as an ordinary human child; it must remain quietly in force in Book 2, not dropped.
- **Health cover (the public explanation for her strangeness):** "photosensitive albinism" — a rare inherited/genetic skin condition with light sensitivity that "even on cloudy days" gives her migraines. Props: tinted glasses, a hat pulled low, a scarf, face turned slightly away, kept out of harsh light. This explains the pale skin, the silver/mirror eyes (behind glasses), and her avoidance of light.
- **Language cover:** "still learning our language" / "doesn't speak much English" / mother from a remote village overseas; she "communicates in her own way, like music." Covers her displaced speech, chirps, and humming.
- **Biometric cover:** the forged medical file (via [[tom]]) deliberately "covered the blinking" and "covered the temp" — i.e., pre-explains her rare blinking and odd body temperature "for a lazy county nurse."
- **Legal identity:** name **Lyra Silvan**, born out west (Colorado), homeschooled; presented in town as James's distant family — a deceased cousin's daughter ("her mother, my cousin, passed; I'm all she had left"). Fake mother: **Mira Silvan, 34** (forged obituary). Papers (birth cert, social, guardianship release, homeschool affidavit + transcripts) forged by [[tom]] and delivered by secure courier-drone drops. Clothes originally from James's niece (box "Rean's Things").
- **Medical anchor:** [[evelyn-roan]] is Lyra's declared physician and advocate — "the only licensed one in this county who's examined her records" — the professional cover that lends the story weight.

Daughter of King Theron and Queen Elara Elenai; survived the destruction of the Celestis Vanaar via a hidden pod that drifted to Earth by accident. Apparent age 6; true age unknown — physiologically stabilized in stasis. She doesn't know Karix, but *feels* him when he turns his gaze toward Earth — a dark resonance that claws at her dreams.

- Who she chooses to become (child, bridge, or something greater).
- The pendant has no standalone codex entry; reveal pacing is Stephen's.
- **The cover story is a live thread, not a closed one.** Book 1 leaves the CPS scrutiny ([[lena-marsh]]) unresolved — Lyra's own line: "She will try again. Questions don't sleep." In Book 2 the pressure escalates from county to federal scale as the cordon converges (Act 2A). Keep the cover quietly present (glasses/hat, "Silvan," Evelyn as physician) even though the seal makes new paperwork from [[tom]] hard to come by.
Book 1, *The Silence Between Stars* (Lyra; the pod, pendant, biosensor wall; First Voice name). Merged from former long-form character source (2026-06-16).

entries.name: Marie

sections.body: The co-op cashier in Pine Valley; a fixture Garrett has bought coffee from for twenty years. In the sealed-in days she gives him a look she never has before — the town's quiet expectation that the badge should have answers.
[Not described.]
Wordless in her scene; the unspoken "You're supposed to know."
[New in Book 2; no Book 1 appearance.]

entries.name: Mason Garrett

fields.label: Affiliation
fields.value: Pine Valley Sheriff's Department (becomes Sheriff in Book 2)

fields.label: Role
fields.value: Deputy; voice of reason; slow-burning ally

sections.body: Deputy under Sheriff Alan Matthews and the quiet backbone of the local department; first name **Mason** (Book 1 prints only "Garrett"). More observant than he lets on, he becomes the first local authority figure to seriously question the official story about James and Lyra. Becomes Sheriff in Book 2.
Unassuming: clean-shaven, short brown hair, calm voice. Doesn't carry himself like a hero, but doesn't flinch when things go sideways. Watchful, honest, quietly sharp eyes.
Thoughtful, measured, slow to conclusions. Believes in evidence and protecting the vulnerable; strong moral compass without naïveté. Haunted by the cost of silence, not by control.
Doesn't know what Lyra is, but senses she's not the threat people think. Recognizes fear-mongering and refuses to be a tool of panic.

Once admired Matthews; watches his unraveling with growing concern, walking a careful line between respect and concern. Initially suspicious of James, he comes to see the quiet decency in James's actions. Feels protective of Pine Valley — and knows protecting the town sometimes means protecting it from itself.
- His arc from deputy to Sheriff and possible bridge between James and the outside world.
Book 1 (Deputy Garrett); first name Mason; becomes Sheriff in Book 2. Merged from former long-form character source (2026-06-16). Naming note per style-guide: "Mason Garrett."

threads: His arc from deputy to Sheriff and possible bridge between James and the outside world.

entries.name: Meraith

fields.label: Affiliation
fields.value: Formerly bound to the Dominion Inquisitorium

fields.label: Role
fields.value: Tracker, silencer, corrupted remnant of an ancient harmonic guardian

fields.label: Status (alive/dead)
fields.value: DESTROYED — unmade by the Veilcore's convergence field at the Book 1 climax (its seed in Alan Matthews survives)

sections.body: An ancient resonance construct — once part of the Irix harmonics defense lattice, made to preserve sacred nodes of memory and song, not to kill. Captured by the Dominion and reprogrammed by High Executor Valek Rhun into a predator of resonance. No longer a mind but a sentient pattern responding to resonance stimuli.

A shadow of refracted geometry and failed light — a humanoid outline constantly collapsing and reforming, faceless but for a mask of shifting fractal mirrors etched with shattered resonance glyphs. Near it, sound warps and time feels misaligned.
Not a will but a pattern. Its imposed Dominion function (seek, suppress, erase unauthorized resonance) clashes with a buried Irix preservation seed, causing instability — especially near Lyra.

Abilities: resonance tracking, phasing movement, silencing field, residual harmonic feedback (hallucinations, memory gaps), node interference. Before destruction it planted a "small, imperceptible, carefully tuned" seed in Sheriff Alan Matthews that "would grow."

Once an Irix guardian; torn from a shattered node centuries ago; deployed (alongside Unit-9F) to Earth and unmade in the clearing. Once served to protect Veilcore-like structures; now its presence threatens to wake or destroy them, and the Earth Veilcore seems to recognize it and resist. Near old Irix nodes or fragments its presence often causes system failures, false awakenings, or protective pulses.
- The seed in Matthews is Meraith's surviving legacy and a live Book 2 thread (fed and steered by [[rhel-vasik]]).
Book 1 (Meraith; reprogrammed by Valek Rhun; unmade at the climax; the seed in Matthews); Book 2 (the seed fed/steered by Vasik). Merged from former long-form character source (2026-06-16).

threads: The seed in Matthews is Meraith's surviving legacy and a live Book 2 thread (fed and steered by [[rhel-vasik]]).

entries.name: Pastor Lindqvist

fields.label: Role
fields.value: Pine Valley pastor; the "closed sky is a mercy" voice when the town's faith splits

sections.body: Pine Valley's pastor. After the Sol Lock seals the sky, his congregation splits down the middle in the second week of the seal: Lindqvist preaches that the closed sky is a mercy — "a hand laid over them, sheltering" — and the dissenting half walks out. The "we are all neighbors here" line on the co-op's rationing list was his idea.
[Not described.]
Reaches for shelter and reassurance; frames the catastrophe as protection.
[New in Book 2; no Book 1 appearance.]
- Where his half of the congregation goes as the cordon closes (Ch. 12+).

entries.name: Rav Seridan

fields.label: Affiliation
fields.value: Dominion — serves within High Executor Valek Rhun's command tier

sections.body: The Irix operative of Book 1: one of the cloaked Irix covert-commanders attending High Executor Valek Rhun in the Dominion command well — "its voice like air escaping crystal." Seridan confirms the royal cadence detected from Sol-3 ("Organic resonance. Thal'Seran. Not debris. Not decay. A voice."), setting the Dominion's hunt for Lyra in motion.
As an Irix: a cloaked, faceless outline; a salvaged distributed intelligence operating under the Dominion Pact, moving through resonance rather than space.
"Voice like air escaping crystal."
Confirmed the Thal'Seran "voice" from Earth — the trigger for the Dominion's pursuit of Lyra. Little else established on-page.
[not stated beyond Book 1 appearance]
- Whether Seridan was connected to Meraith and Unit-9F's deployment, or how it regards their unmaking on Earth (open for Book 2).

Book 1 (Rav Seridan, the Irix operative confirming the Thal'Seran "voice" from Sol-3). Merged from former long-form character source (2026-06-16). entries.name: Rhel Vasik

fields.label: Role
fields.value: Strategist; phantom tactician; resonance analyst; Book 2's human-scale antagonist (the enemy inside the Sol Lock)

sections.body: A Syntari intelligence strategist (~100 standard cycles) who came to Sol to *quantify* the anomaly and got sealed inside the lock with it. A "phantom tactician" who shapes a battlefield before the first shot. Where Enlarn knocks at the wall from outside, Vasik is the enemy *within* the lock. (Name settled per Stephen, June 2026 — formerly the "[NEW NAME TBD]" Syntari strategist, distinct from the Book 1 Irix operative [[rav-seridan]].)

Synthetic flesh that mimics normal skin; faintly luminescent eyes with data-thread pupils that flicker near resonance; favors nondescript clothing ("invisibility by normalcy"). True form sleek and insectile, adaptive plating, unornamented.

Coldly curious, analytical to the point of sociopathy; individuals are irrelevant — only data, deviation, and resonance potential matter. Believes in the Dominion's ordered galaxy but does not *worship* it (unlike Karix or Rhun); for Vasik order is a game of logic. Speaks softly, never wastes a motion.

Toolkit: neural-interface mapping (reads/steers weak or unshielded minds at short range), tactical projection, infiltration (synthetic identities, voice masks), resonance analysis, and **cognitive partitioning** (mind split across sealed layers — resistant to psychic intrusion). Earth fascinates him because it sings *off-key*, breaking his predictive models.

Drawn in partly "after the death of a Dominion asset (Solane)" — an open thread (see [[solane-sleeper-network]]). He is the machine that feels nothing, steering the man who feels too much (Matthews). Stranded when the lock seals, his mission inverts: trapped with the thing he came to measure, unable to report out. In Act 3 — unable to open the door himself — he makes his play with the suppression doctrine distilled: a parasitic counter-key jacked into the deep interface, holding the Great Song hostage. He loses not to force but to the convergence made physical (James). He never knows *why* he loses: he and the seed needed Lyra *opened*, not taken, and the lock answers to the convergence, not to Lyra alone — so the moment his plan opens the way down, it has already failed, delivering the child to the one place her bond with James becomes decisive. The deep then makes his sealed partitions sing to each other, and he feels one thing, once — ending the book broken open, unprecedented, and alive.

- **Reveal discipline:** the Ch. 9 "he's feeding the seed" beat is the turn — DELIVERED in Ch. 9: he finds Meraith's seed in Matthews (via the four-note hum), discards excising/reporting, and feeds/steers it through his neural interface as an inside antenna mapping the lock + the path to the deep.
- **Partition-instability plant (Ch. 9):** with the Dominion lattice cut off by the Sol Lock, his cognitive partitions hold themselves apart without external reference; in Ch. 9 two of them brush into an almost-chord and he files it as "instrument drift." Planted payoff: Ch. 24/28 (partitions sing to each other) → Ch. 36 ("feel one thing, once").
- The Solane hook (reveal vs. retire — Solane unnamed in Book 1). Touched lightly in Ch. 9: Solane's death drew him to Sol and he never understood it; cause still undefined.
- The federal cover (Ch. 9): a clean federal identity he means to ride into the cordon. Note: this is *separate* from the Ch. 10 "[[the-soft-man]]," who is a surviving [[solane-sleeper-network]] sleeper, not Vasik (ruling, Stephen 2026-06-15). Open: whether the two ever make contact or work the same ground unaware.
- Act 3: he loses to the convergence made physical (James); the deep makes him "feel one thing, once" — a Book 3 engine.

Book 2 (Rhel Vasik; the Ch. 9 reveal, the descent, the Act 3 poisoning, the broken-open ending); the Syntari species profile. Merged from former long-form character source (2026-06-16). Name settled per Stephen, June 2026 (Vasik is the strategist; Valek Rhun a separate character).

entries.name: Sera

fields.label: Role
fields.value: Field archivist; first to surface the *Celestis Vanaar* record discrepancy on the Accord side

sections.body: The Kelari archivist who, during the post-recognition unsealing of the Elenai dossier, flags an inconsistency in the sealed survey of the *Celestis Vanaar*'s fall: the harmonic data does not match the official account — "not by much; by enough." Her instinct ("we do not misremember by enough") plants, on the Accord side of the wall, the same crime that [[kelar-vex]] carries inside it. Distinct from Vex: Sera remains outside, on Virellis Prime, working the record through official channels.

Kelari: chitinous, mandibled, antennae and compound eyes. Reads as careful, precise, faintly agitated when the record is wrong — a small mandible-rhythm Elyon reads as the Kelari equivalent of a frown.
Careful, precise; the careful agitation of a Kelari who has found a falsified record (falsification = cultural death to her kind).
First to surface the Vanaar record discrepancy on the Accord side. Female (she/her), per Ch. 5.
[not stated beyond Book 2 introduction]

- Reveal discipline: Book 2 names the *crime*, not the *hand* (the present-day Old Fracture individual is a Book 3 reveal).

entries.name: Shade-Lord Myra Venn
entries.detail: Unknown (formerly Virellan; heavily altered through forbidden resonance manipulation)

fields.label: Species
fields.value: Unknown (formerly Virellan; heavily altered through forbidden resonance manipulation)

fields.label: Affiliation
fields.value: Independent (aligned with Dominion interests, not under direct command)

fields.label: Role
fields.value: Rogue harmonic manipulator; resonance heretic; prophet of collapse

sections.body: A myth among resonance scholars: once a Virellan scholar of deep harmonics who fractured — biologically, spiritually, dimensionally — reaching into the theoretical "Discord Layer" of the First Voice. She returned changed: "neither dead, nor sane." Calls herself a mirror. In Book 1 she operates alongside Dominion command by choice, conferring with Admiral Voss Enlarn and personally overseeing the Meraith deployment ("Shadowfall commences within the week").

Tall, angular, wrapped in layered robes of shifting resonance fibers. Eyes glow with layered light "as if each glance reflects alternate timelines." Pale skin with faint circuit-like harmonics. Voice is layered — a whisper and a wail in parallel.

Oscillates between true madness (speech dissolving into riddles, half First Voice, half poetic fragments) and long stretches of cold, predatory lucidity. The oscillation is the point — those who deal with her never know which Myra will answer.

Believes the universe is collapsing harmonically into noise and sees herself as its herald/reflection — she does not want power, she wants witnesses, believing the song is ending and someone must hear the last verse. Calls Lyra "the untouched note" / "the last note that remembers the first"; James "the convergence, the fire-sleeper, the unwound chord," whom she both fears and pities. First Voice mastery (speaks multiple harmonic threads at once, can induce visions or break weak minds), fractured presence, resonance bending, echo imprinting, and Veil Sense — drawn to Veilcore structures but cannot enter without permission; they reject her, yet remember her. She believes in fate, but "fate that loops, folds, and bleeds."

"Shade-Lord" is self-bestowed, not a formal Dominion rank. Has possibly met High Executor Valek Rhun once (neither speaks of it; Rhun calls her only a "threat vector unclassifiable"). May be distantly related to Aelri Venn-Orin ("echo-kin"; Verne fears her and is warned not to listen too closely). May once have been part of the Veilcore's design, or at least witnessed its early harmonics — it remembers her but does not trust her.

- Whether Lyra stabilizes the song or ends it (Myra refuses to say).
- Possible kinship with Aelri Venn-Orin.
Book 1 (Shade-Lord Myra Venn; conferring with Enlarn and overseeing the Meraith deployment). Merged from former long-form character source (2026-06-16).

entries.name: The "Soft Man" (surviving Solane sleeper) [NAME TBD]

fields.label: Role
fields.value: The last low-grade Solane sleeper still active inside the cordon; the Dominion's "face in the cordon too composed"

sections.body: The advance federal interrogator who comes down to question the Pine Valley sheriff's department in the third week of the seal — soft-spoken, in a good coat, with credentials Garrett (twenty years of knowing what real ones feel like) can find nothing wrong with and trusts not at all. **Ruling (Stephen, 2026-06-15): he is a surviving Solane sleeper, not Rhel Vasik's federal cover.** He is the network's "face in the cordon a little too composed" — the enemy already home, wearing the colors of the response. Distinct from Vasik, who maintains his own separate federal cover (Ch. 9).
Soft-spoken; a good coat; forgettable by design. Never raises his voice or his eyebrows.

Mild, patient, frictionless. Asks good questions gently — the back roads, the people who've lived here longest, the family past the tree line "that drew local attention over the winter." Knew Garrett's name though Garrett was nearly sure he'd never given it.
Working the cordon's interest toward James and Lyra. Open question whether he is coordinating with Vasik or running on stale standing sleeper doctrine (see [[solane-sleeper-network]]).
Placed before the Veilcore's awakening as part of the pre-existing Solane network; sealed inside with everyone when the Sol Lock fell.

- His name and species — undefined on the page.
- Whether he and Vasik ever make contact, or work the same ground unaware of each other.
- His fate as the cordon hardens. In Ch. 12 he reappears inside the established cordon — asks Garrett again about the fire road, writes nothing, and reads as running "a chain of his own" (i.e., not taking orders up the federal chain). Keeps his hand invisible to the characters.
Manuscript CHAPTER_10; ruling per Stephen 2026-06-15. Corroborated by solane-sleeper-network ("a face in the federal cordon a little too composed").

entries.name: King Theron Elenai
entries.firstApp: Book 1 (in Lyra's memory / Veilcore vision; died pre-Chapter 1)

fields.label: Role
fields.value: Final sovereign of House Elenai; father of Lyra; steward (not architect) of the Veilcore

fields.label: First appearance
fields.value: Book 1 (in Lyra's memory / Veilcore vision; died pre-Chapter 1)

fields.label: Status (alive/dead)
fields.value: DECEASED — died aboard the *Celestis Vanaar* in the Dominion ambush

sections.body: The last sovereign of House Elenai, the Thal'Seran ruling lineage. A ruler of unshakable calm who saw the Thal'Seran as stewards of harmonic balance, not rulers. Betrayed from within and ambushed by the Dominion, Theron died aboard the *Celestis Vanaar* alongside Queen Elara — his last on-page moments seen through Lyra's eyes (a kiss on her forehead, a gaze locked across a wall of flame, a command she couldn't hear).

Tall, dignified, robed in woven resonance light. Eyes shimmering with layered memory. Bore no metal crown — only the sigil of his house etched in living light across his palms, pulsing with his bloodline's signature.
Thought before action; words carrying weight beyond sound. In him resonance flowed rather than thundered. His great flaw was hope.
Encoded the Veilcore's final harmonics into his daughter's blood and scattered the legacy. Knew the Thal'Seran had hidden the Earth Veilcore for a reason beyond strategy.

Crowned amid encroaching darkness; chose to plant a seed (Lyra) rather than wage unwinnable war. Did not raise Lyra for war but for memory, believing she would one day remember enough to choose her own path. Lyra's pod reached Earth by accident, not his design. Queen Elara was his bondmate and equal — his compass, as he was her shield. Never stopped hoping Kael would return to the light, but never let that hope blind him. Saw Karix as a force of entropy — not evil but hollow, a being who erased himself for power.
- Whether "something ancient sang from Earth" beyond strategy (series keeps this open).
Book 1 (King Theron Elenai; House Elenai / the Fall). Merged from former long-form character source (2026-06-16). Per canon rule #6: stewards, not architects; Lyra reaching Earth was an accident.

entries.name: Tom

fields.label: Role
fields.value: Off-page fixer/forger who manufactured Lyra's legal identity and medical cover

sections.body: An old military friend of James's — James "pulled [his] ass out of that hole in Kandahar," a debt Tom repays by building Lyra a paper existence, no questions asked beyond what he needs. He produces the documents that make the cover story real: birth certificate, social, guardianship release, homeschool affidavit and transcripts under a private umbrella, and a grief-worn fake Colorado obituary ("Mira Silvan, 34, survived by her daughter"). His second packet also "covered the blinking, covered the temp" — falsified medical records to explain Lyra's non-human tells "for a lazy county nurse." Delivers by secure courier-drone drops (e.g., the hollow of a dead tree off the ridge trail).
[Never on-page; voice on a phone, handwriting on a folded note.]
Dry, loyal, discreet. "Enough to calm the right eyes and confuse the wrong ones." Doesn't ask more than he needs to.
Knows Lyra has no real records and that James is hiding something; does not know what she is. The single outside human James trusts with the identity problem.
Served with James (Kandahar). Otherwise undefined.

- His reachability under the Sol Lock — the seal's comms chaos may cut the drop pipeline that the cover depended on. Open whether he resurfaces in Book 2 or the cover simply has to hold on what already exists.
Book 1 (the forging arc: "Lyra Silvan," the Kandahar debt, the courier-drop packets, "Covered the blinking. Covered the temp."); Manuscript Book 1 reference text.

entries.name: The Deep Structure (Canadian Shield Descent)
entries.firstApp: Book 2, Act 2B (descent set-piece, Ch. 21–30 per plan)

fields.label: First appearance
fields.value: Book 2, Act 2B (descent set-piece, Ch. 21–30 per plan)

sections.body: The physical way down to the Deep Root: the oldest exposed bedrock on the continent (the **Canadian Shield**), reachable from Pine Valley and entered through **Iron Range mine access** (confirmed literal per Stephen). Not a portal but a place — the descent party drives to a mine and goes "into the bones of the world." The route is geologically sound, not convenient: the Iron Range sits on Shield bedrock, and generations of iron mining have already driven shafts and pits deep into it, so humans cut the first stretch of the road down without ever knowing what it led toward. This entry is the *place* (the shell); the Root itself lives in [[the-deep-root]].

Three-stage descent, deliberately eroding the reader's footing: ordinary mine workings → natural fault/cave systems older than any tool → the deep structure proper. Down where the Root resonates the surroundings read as **drowned cathedral** (vast, water-shaped, reverent, wrong) and **Irix-adjacent geometries** (angles that don't resolve, spaces larger inside than their approach allowed, surfaces grown or *sung* rather than built). Impressionistic, never a blueprint or floor plan (per Stephen).

- The Act 2B descent set-piece; revelation chamber (Ch. 29–30) holds the Deep Root (the site is the shell; the Root is the occupant). The descent is tuned to make the Root's nature land: the further from sunlight and the works of hands, the closer to the oldest living instrument of the Song.
- Meraith's seed can find the way down because the buried Irix guardian-ancestry is a map to exactly this kind of door (the midpoint raid "opens the way down"). The Irix were "too foundational for rumor" and once guarded such sites; the Irix-adjacent feel of the deep architecture is not coincidence but the resonance the Irix were once keyed to, still here, older than their guardianship.

- Deliberately NOT Thal'Seran construction — it predates them; the Veilcore was a graft onto this, not the reverse (Deep Root).
- Whether anything here was "built" at all stays ambiguous — may be the shape the Root's presence pressed into rock over an unfathomable span.

- A name for the site — none established; can stay descriptive ("the deep places") or take an in-world designation (couples to whether the Root is named).
- The mine-to-natural handoff — exact transition point (single legacy shaft vs. pit floor breaching a cavern) undecided.
- Hazards of the descent (effect on instruments, light, time, unshielded minds) to be set when chapters are outlined, consistent with what the lock/Root do to perception elsewhere.
- Reveal discipline (Book 2): the descent is the book's strange heart — wondrous and unsettling in equal measure, never a dungeon crawl; pace the unreality (ordinary mine, then old caves, then the impossible) so the reader's footing erodes with the party's.

- Book 2 plan (Canadian Shield / Iron Range geology; drowned-cathedral / Irix-adjacent descent set-piece; Ch. 29–30 revelation chamber); Lore_The_Deep_Root.md; Lore_Meraiths_Seed.md. Merged from former long-form worldbuilding source (2026-06-16).

entries.name: Dot's (the diner)
entries.firstApp: Book 2, Chapter 12 (named); the unnamed "diner" recurs Ch. 4, Ch. 10

fields.label: First appearance

sections.body: The Pine Valley diner — the town's gathering place and rumor exchange. Its one television (the set that still pulls the ghosting federal loop through the lensed EM, Ch. 4 / Ch. 10) makes it where the town goes to find out what's happening to it. In Ch. 12 it's where the town audibly splits over "the Carter girl" — Hal Owens voicing the fear, Marie the protectiveness — while Garrett listens at the counter.
Counter, stools, booths; a TV in the corner; the ordinary warmth of a small-town diner against the wrong sky outside.
The civic temperature gauge of Pine Valley. Where fear and protectiveness of Lyra first come to open words (Ch. 12).
Inside the Sol Lock and (from Ch. 12) inside the federal cordon.
- Whether "Dot" is a character on the page or only the diner's name (unestablished).
Manuscript CHAPTER_12 (named "Dot's"); CHAPTER_04, CHAPTER_10 (the diner, its television).

entries.name: Earth / Sol System

sections.body: Earth (Sol-System, Planet 3) — a terrestrial world the galaxy dismissed as primitive but which, impossibly, "sings": its harmonic baseline is stable across deep time and warps probability gracefully. The Thal'Seran hid a Veilcore here long before Elenai rule; beneath it lies the Deep Root. Designated a Red-Zone Containment World by the Accord (Decree 472.88, ~four centuries ago).

Ordinary blue world; ~9.4 billion sapients (unverified per Accord docs). The change in Book 2 is the sky: external starlight is lensed crossing the heliopause shell, so the night sky sits "subtly wrong" — shifted, doubled at the limb, dimmer stars smeared out (Sol Lock; CHAPTER_06: stars "smear and double").

- The origin-site of the Great Song's deepest root; the cradle, not the author (Deep Root).
- Chosen by House Elenai for the Veilcore because it "sang" with unshaped human emotion (Why Earth Was Chosen; Hidden Harmony).
- In Book 2, sealed inside [[the-sol-lock]] at heliopause scale (~120 AU), FTL-dead within; the Spear Fleet arrives at "a door that wasn't there" (Ch. 1).

- Sol Lock: no resonance-based FTL travel or comms anywhere inside the heliopause; sublight matter and ordinary EM still pass (EM lensed at the shell). One-way inbound micro-fracture leakage of FTL signal (rare).
- Accord containment directives: contact/trade prohibited, observation only under veil protocols.

- Accord reconsideration of Earth's classification (GREY-OBSERVE) pending council vote (in-world docs — deliberately fallible).
- Whether anything deeper than coincidence drew Lyra here stays open (Why Earth Was Chosen).
- Manuscript CHAPTER_06. Merged from former long-form source (2026-06-16).

threads: Accord reconsideration of Earth's classification (GREY-OBSERVE) pending council vote (in-world docs — deliberately fallible).

entries.name: Federal Staging Ground (the old airstrip)
entries.detail: region (federal land north of Pine Valley)

fields.label: Scale
fields.value: region (federal land north of Pine Valley)

fields.label: Controlled by
fields.value: US federal government / the response (the gathering cordon)

sections.body: The federal land at the top of the county and the old airstrip north of Pine Valley, taken over in the third week of the seal as the government's response converges on the Arrowhead epicenter. Convoys roll north through town and disappear into the trees; a camp with lights and generators goes up in woods that held nothing but deer Garrett's whole life. The staging ground for the federal cordon that closes around Pine Valley (foreshadowed Ch. 10; arrives Ch. 12).
North of town, up the county road, on federal land and an old airstrip; through Garrett's lens, a new glow on the dark treeline — generators, a camp — where there had never been light before.

The physical sign that Pine Valley's anonymity is over. The convoys take it first, then the airstrip; the advance "[[the-soft-man]]" — a surviving Solane sleeper (ruling, Stephen 2026-06-15) — works out of the response that bases here.
Inside the Sol Lock with everyone else — sealed in, no FTL/comms out (locked decision #1).

- In Ch. 12 the staging ground becomes an operational **cordon**: overnight it throws out checkpoints on the three roads in and a line of trucks/antennas along the ridgelines, ringing the valley. Open: whether it is formally the cordon HQ and how it tightens toward the cabin in Ch. 13+.
Manuscript CHAPTER_10.

entries.name: The Hollow Drift
entries.firstApp: Backstory (the Shattering); referenced as deep history

fields.label: First appearance
fields.value: Backstory (the Shattering); referenced as deep history

sections.body: The resonance scar left by the Thal'Seran's failed Infinite Convergence — "not a void, but a resonance scar, where existence forgets itself." Born when a misalignment in the outer lattice fractured reality during the Convergence Project (possibly thousands of years before the Accord and Dominion). The wound at the center of the Thal'Seran fall.

A "fractured chord, endlessly trying to resolve." Space-folding fails there because the quantum field is recursive — coordinates loop, identity shifts mid-transit, even thought unravels. Some say it still faintly "sings" — a half-song with no ending.

- Where the Thal'Seran were lost to "dissonant unmaking"; only the Celestis Vanaar (House Elenai) escaped.
- Karix later found a shard of the Infinite Convergence seed in a decayed node on the Drift's edge, and a cryptic seed labeled 17-K bearing the Elenai crest (Vol Karix lore).
A place reality cannot resolve; FTL/space-folding fails within it. Recreating the convergence might tear the wound open again — a reason House Elenai hid.
- The Drift's lingering "half-song" and whether it can ever resolve is left open in the lore.
- Backstory (the Shattering / Thal'Seran fall). Merged from former long-form source (2026-06-16).

entries.name: James's Cabin

sections.body: James Carter's home outside Pine Valley, where he raises Lyra after the crash. The story's domestic heart — kitchen-table scenes, the place where the ordinary and the cosmic collide.
A back-roads cabin: percolator, woodstove, kitchen window looking out on pines and the (now wrong) night sky. Reached by a track over ruts. Intimate, lived-in, snowbound (CHAPTER_02, CHAPTER_06).

- Where James and Lyra live; site of quiet character beats and the dawning realization that the sky has become a wall.
- "The last night at the cabin, when the sky began to listen back" (CHAPTER_08) — the cabin is the vantage from which the lock's effects are first felt domestically.
Under [[the-sol-lock]]; the Veilcore's waking pulse disabled satellite TV, GPS, radio, and FTL comms only (fading over 1–2 weeks), ordinary machinery untouched (CHAPTER_02 locked note).
- None specific recorded.
- Manuscript CHAPTER_02 (percolator, woodstove, GPS, the track/clearing), CHAPTER_06 (kitchen window, wrong sky), CHAPTER_08.

sections.body: A volcanic super-terrestrial world (Sythra Arm // Planet 9) with dense toxic atmosphere and subterranean caves, home to the proto-industrial lithovore **Khessari**. Tier-2 Grey-Zone Observation World under Accord Decree 601.45. Source is a deliberately-fallible Accord file.
Volcanic, toxic atmosphere, sprawling cave networks; geothermal engineering and crystal-based data storage by the Khessari.

- Holds Thal'Seran "conduction spires" embedded deep in the mantle, considered sacred by the Khessari; a "world-pulse" likely a Veilcore echo.
- Some field observers report that Khessari song-prayers bear structural similarity to recorded First Voice fragments (Cultural Interpreter review pending).
- Author-side significance: the Deep Root entry notes Khessara as a node-world carrying its own deep stratum, "beginning to wake and answer" — a Book 3 thread.
Grey-Zone observation; mantle instability means tampering with spires risks planetary-scale eruptions.

- Native factions divided over whether the "world-pulse" should be awakened (per document — unverified).
- Khessara as another deep-root node-world is held for Book 3 reveal (Deep Root reveal discipline) — do not resolve.

entries.name: Pine Valley

fields.label: Controlled by
fields.value: none (human civic — Pine Valley sheriff's office)

sections.body: The small town in northern Minnesota where Lyra's escape pod fell and where James Carter lives. The story's human center of gravity — the town that "didn't know yet that its sky was a wall." Naming note (locked): the town is **Pine Valley**; "Pine Ridge" is retired.

Snowbound northern-Minnesota country: pines, back roads the snow erases, a sheriff's office with a duty log and a co-op line. Near the Iron Range and the Canadian Shield bedrock (see deep-structure descent). Pine Hollow is a nearby area, not part of the town proper.

- Crash/convergence site (the clearing) lies near the town; the Veilcore beneath Earth woke here through Lyra.
- Book 2's siege-from-orbit and intimate horror (Meraith's seed in Sheriff Matthews) both play out here.
- Mason Garrett becomes Sheriff in Book 2.

Under [[the-sol-lock]] like all of Earth. The Veilcore's initial sealing pulse knocked out satellite TV, GPS, and radio locally (fading over 1–2 weeks; CHAPTER_02 locked note); ordinary machinery and cars untouched.
- None specific recorded; town's fate tied to the lock's lifespan (Sol Lock open dials).

sections.body: The Pine Valley clinic run by Dr. Evelyn Roan. Naming locked: the clinic is the **Roan Family Medical Clinic** (the sign reads "Roan Family Medicine"). Where Lyra is examined and the resonance phenomena are first measured against medical instruments.
Small-town clinic: monitors, a calm green line walking left to right, the smell of yellow tea, afternoon light slanting gold through pine (CHAPTER_07).

- Lyra's check-ups happen here; in CHAPTER_07 she registers a strike on the [[the-sol-lock]] in her body ("Someone is pressing the sky… It comes down a wire") seconds before instruments would.
- A point where the human/medical world and the cosmic phenomena intersect.
[None special to the place beyond standard clinic; resonance effects are Lyra's, not the building's.]

sections.body: A lush oceanic world (Hesserin Reach // Planet 4), home to the amphibious **Therrin** (early-maritime culture, oral history, ocean-sculptures). Classified Tier-3 Amber-Zone Cultural Reserve under Accord Decree 312.19. Source is a deliberately-fallible Accord intelligence file — treat claims as conjecture.
Scattered archipelagos on a lush ocean world; monumental ocean-sculptures; "songstorms" (oceanic harmonic surges).

- Carries reports of submerged crystalline structures matching ancient Thal'Seran geometry; Therrin practice long-wave harmonic ocean navigation possibly of deep-time off-world origin. A seed for the wider "Thal'Seran relics across many worlds" motif.
Amber-Zone: limited observation permitted to preserve cultural purity.
- If submerged structures prove Thal'Seran, classification may quietly upgrade to Tier 2 (per the document — unverified).
- In-world Accord planetary profile (deliberately fallible). Merged from former long-form source (2026-06-16).

threads: If submerged structures prove Thal'Seran, classification may quietly upgrade to Tier 2 (per the document — unverified).

entries.name: The Clearing

sections.body: The crash/convergence site near James's cabin — the wooded clearing where the Book 1 climax played out and where the Veilcore woke through Lyra. Load-bearing: "The Veilcore did [close the sky], the night of the clearing" (CHAPTER_06).
A clearing at the edge of the cabin's track, crossed on foot; ringed by pines. James "watched the light come down through his daughter in the clearing like current finding a wire" (CHAPTER_02).

- Site of the convergence: the Veilcore reached for the world through Lyra here, and the [[the-sol-lock]] sealed Sol the same night.
- "It wasn't only her, in the clearing" (CHAPTER_06) — James's emotional resonance was also present (James as second key; see Deep Root).
- Where Meraith was unmade by the Veilcore and planted its seed in Sheriff Matthews ([[meraiths-seed]]).
- "The clearing chose her" (CHAPTER_06).
Resonance behavior here is extreme: the convergence event and Veilcore awakening occurred at this spot.
- The full nature of what happened "in the clearing" with James (the convergence two-key reveal) is an Act 2B/Book 2 reveal — do not resolve.
- Manuscript CHAPTER_02, CHAPTER_06. Merged from former long-form source (2026-06-16).

sections.body: An ice-covered rogue planetoid (Aris Belt // Planetoid Theta-14) adrift between Accord sectors, believed a former Syntari biotechnical archive world lost during the Dominion's expansion wars. No native population. Tier-1 Red-Zone Containment World under Accord Decree 890.22. Source is a deliberately-fallible Accord file.
Ice-covered, irregular gravimetric drift; deep-core scans reveal partially active biological computation structures.

- Declared Red-Zone after two Accord research corvettes' crews suffered catastrophic neural collapse from ambient biospheric harmonics. Possible link to abandoned Syntari consciousness-transfer experiments.
Red-Zone containment; an unknown harmonic signal causes rapid neural resonance overload in unshielded sapients.
- Covert Accord rumor: the harmonic signature has "changed twice in the past two decades, as if responding to an external presence" (per document — unverified).
- In-world Accord planetary profile (deliberately fallible). Merged from former long-form source (2026-06-16).

entries.name: Virellis Prime

sections.body: The standard venue and seat of the Accord Council (naming locked: **Virellis Prime**). A major Virellan-settled world. Where the galaxy's political response to Earth, the lock, and the Elenai question is debated.

A Virellan world — by species profile, radiant skies and light-plains; cities "grown" from crystalline vines and photosensitive matrices, responsive to mood and collective intention (see [[virellans]]).

- Seat of the Accord Council; the Council also convenes elsewhere (e.g., Oralis V).
- Setting for Book 2 chamber/political scenes (CHAPTER_05, Elyon POV); where Sera surfaces the Celestis Vanaar record discrepancy (Ch. 5).
[None special recorded.]

- Continuity: RESOLVED (2026-06-15) — Book 1 was revised to "Virellis Prime"; the earlier "Virelia Prime" has been removed. (Open ASSUMPTION: Seran'Tir as Virellan homeworld with Virellis Prime a settled council world — confirm with Stephen.)
- Book 1 (council scenes); Manuscript CHAPTER_05. Merged from former long-form source (2026-06-16).
